auth.setupDescription: Set up the administrator account for Bridge.
auth.loginDescription: Enter your credentials to access Bridge.
auth.loginDescriptionSSO: Sign in with your organization account.
auth.orUseCredentials: or sign in with username and password

host.title: Hosts
host.scanAllDescription: Sequentially test and capture specs from all active hosts with credentials configured.
host.noTopologyDesc: Link this host to DNS records, services, or projects to see its topology.
host.sshConfigHint: Paste this block into your ~/.ssh/config file to enable quick access via: ssh {slug}
host.noAlertsDesc: This host has no active alerts.
host.noChamadosDesc: No active tickets for this host.
host.sshKeyKeepCurrentHint: Leaving this unchanged keeps the key currently linked to this host.
host.sshKeyClearHint: Saving will remove the key from this host. You can re-link one later.
host.testKeyDisabledNoKey: No SSH key linked to this host.
host.testPasswordDisabledNoPassword: No password stored for this host.
host.noResponsaveisDesc: No responsible people registered for this host.
host.emptyStateAdd: Add your first host to get started

service.title: Services
service.fixateDesc: Pin this service so it persists even if the container goes down

sshConfig.title: SSH Config
sshConfig.generateDescription: Download or copy the SSH config generated from active hosts.
sshConfig.writeWarning: This will overwrite the SSH config file on the server.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noScansYet: No scans yet. Run a scan to see activity here.

issue.title: Issues
issue.noIssuesDesc: Create an issue to track work for this host.

tool.title: Tools
tool.name: Name
tool.embedBlockedTitle: This application could not be embedded
tool.embedBlockedDescription: The application blocked iframe loading or did not complete session bootstrap within the expected time.
tool.embedLikelyBlockedMinio: MinIO commonly blocks iframe embedding via security headers (X-Frame-Options/CSP). Open externally or adjust the proxy to allow frame-ancestors.
tool.embedLikelyUnstableAirflow: Airflow loaded, but iframe sessions may fail without base_url, proxy headers, and cookie settings aligned.

serviceCredentials.title: Application Credentials
serviceCredentials.subtitle: Encrypted credentials for services and applications
serviceCredentials.noServicesDesc: Add credentials to a service to see them here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.appNameDescription: The name displayed in the sidebar, login page, and browser tab.
settings.mainColorDescription: The primary accent color used throughout the interface.
settings.logoDescription: Upload a custom logo (max 512KB). Displayed in the sidebar and login page.
settings.backupDescription: Download a portable backup (.sshcmbak) of the whole database. The file is cross-dialect: you can restore it into either a SQLite or a Postgres deployment.
settings.restoreDescription: Upload a .sshcmbak file to replace the current database. Backups taken on SQLite can be restored into Postgres and vice versa.
settings.restoreWarning: DANGER: this completely wipes and replaces the current database — every user, host, DNS record, service, project, SSH key and session — with the contents of the backup. You (and everyone else) will be signed out and must log in again with credentials from the backup.
settings.restoreConfirm:
You are about to REPLACE ALL DATA in this instance with the uploaded backup.

This wipes users, hosts, DNS records, services, projects, SSH keys, sessions and every other table. It cannot be undone.

Your current login will stop working — you will be signed out and must log in again with a user from the backup.

Continue?
settings.restoreSuccessLogout: Signing you out in a moment — please log in again with a user from the restored backup.

operation.testPasswordDesc: Verify SSH connectivity using password authentication.
operation.testKeyDesc: Verify SSH connectivity using key-based authentication.
operation.testCaptureDesc: Connect and collect system information (CPU, RAM, disk, services, containers).
operation.setupKeyDesc: Test password connection, then generate and install an SSH key on the remote host.
operation.repairDevNullDesc: Fix broken /dev/null on the remote host (requires sudo).
operation.noCreds: No password or key configured. Add credentials to enable operations.
operation.scanSaved: Scan saved. Empty resource fields auto-updated.
operation.repairTip: Tip: grant sudo for this user (NOPASSWD) or fix /dev/null manually on the remote host.
operation.setPreferredAuth: Host has both password and key. Set preferred auth first before running auto-fix.
operation.confirmRepair: This will run privileged commands on the remote host to repair /dev/null. Continue?
operation.setupSudoNopasswdDesc: Configure passwordless sudo for the remote user (creates /etc/sudoers.d drop-in). Connects via key or password; requires stored password for sudo.
operation.confirmSudoNopasswd: This will grant passwordless sudo to the remote user. This is a security-sensitive operation. Continue?
operation.listRemoteKeysDesc: List SSH keys configured on the remote host (authorized_keys and private keys in ~/.ssh/).
operation.noRemoteKeys: No SSH keys found on the remote host.
operation.dockerSetupDesc: Check Docker and Compose versions, group membership, and optionally fix sudo requirement.
operation.noConsoleOutput: Run an operation to see its output here.
operation.coolifyDisabledDesc: Requires successful SSH key authentication to use Coolify integration.
operation.coolifyCheckDesc: Search Coolify for a server matching this host's IP.
operation.coolifyNotFoundDesc: This host's IP was not found among Coolify servers.
operation.coolifyDeleteConfirm: Remove this server from Coolify? This cannot be undone.
operation.coolifyKeyUpdated: Coolify server now uses the selected key.
operation.coolifyKeyPickerEmpty: No SSH keys available — create one under SSH Keys.
operation.createRemoteUserDesc: Create a new user on the remote host with SSH key access and passwordless sudo.
operation.createRemoteUserFormDesc: Enter a username and select an SSH key. The user will be created with the key in authorized_keys and NOPASSWD sudo.
operation.createRemoteUserInvalidName: Username must start with a letter or underscore, followed by lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores (max 32 chars).
operation.createRemoteUserKeyHint: This key's public part will be added to the new user's ~/.ssh/authorized_keys. Only keys with a stored public key are listed.
operation.createRemoteUserNoEligibleKeys: No SSH keys with a stored public key. Add or generate one under SSH Keys first.
operation.createRemoteUserNoPubKey: Selected key has no public key — pick another.
operation.deleteRemoteUserDesc: Remove a non-system user from the remote host, kill their running processes, and clean up their NOPASSWD sudoers drop-in.
operation.deleteRemoteUserFormDesc: Enter the username to delete. Root and other system accounts (UID < 1000) are blocked. The current SSH login user is also protected.
operation.deleteRemoteUserRemoveHomeHint: Runs userdel with -r, deleting /home/<username> and the user's mail spool. Off by default so you can audit the home directory first.
operation.deleteRemoteUserProtected: This account is a protected system user and cannot be deleted from here.
operation.deleteRemoteUserWarning: This action is destructive: running processes will be killed and the account removed. Make sure no service or cron job depends on this user.
operation.deleteRemoteUserConfirm: Delete remote user "{username}"? This cannot be undone.
operation.deleteRemoteUserSourceScan: Users discovered during the last scan ({date}). Run a capture scan to refresh.
operation.deleteRemoteUserNoScanHint: No scan data available yet. Run a capture scan (Operations → Test & Capture Specs) to populate a user picker here.
operation.nginxCleanupDesc: Detect and remove non-containerized nginx. Backs up config before changes.
operation.confirmNginxCleanup: This will stop and disable the native nginx service on the remote host. A backup of /etc/nginx/ will be created first. Continue?
operation.confirmNginxPurge: Also purge (uninstall) the nginx packages? Click Cancel to just stop & disable without removing packages.

alert.title: Alerts
alert.noAlertsDesc: No active alerts for this host.
alert.manualDesc: Manual alerts are informational notes. Automated alerts are generated from scan data.

scan.noDataDesc: Run a scan from the Operations tab to collect system information.
scan.userNoKeys: No SSH keys found for this user (either the home directory is empty or we lack read access).
